// SLIDE 01 — HOOK

CHAPTER 4 — THE COWORK TASK BRIEF

A task brief is not a longer prompt. It is the specification that makes your intent executable and inspectable before Co

NARRATION

A task brief is not a longer prompt. It is the specification that makes your intent executable and inspectable before Cowork does any work. A good brief has seven fields: objective, allowed sources, exclusions, output artifact, decision rules, review checkpoints, and stop conditions. Vague instruction plus broad access is how Cowork produces fluent, plausible, wrong output. The brief is also your verification standard: if the output does not match the brief, the work is not done.

// SLIDE 02 — WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO

WHAT THIS CHAPTER LETS YOU DO.

·Write a Cowork task brief using a reusable seven-field template.
·Identify what is missing from a vague instruction and why the missing elements matter.
·Apply the brief template to a report task and an extraction task with worked examples.
·Use the brief as the standard against which you review the plan Cowork proposes.
NARRATION

By the end of this chapter you will be able to: Write a Cowork task brief using a reusable seven-field template. Identify what is missing from a vague instruction and why the missing elements matter. Apply the brief template to a report task and an extraction task with worked examples. Use the brief as the standard against which you review the plan Cowork proposes.

// SLIDE 03 — OPENING SCENE

OPENING SCENE

Here is a real type of exchange that happens when professionals start using Cowork. Two people, same folder of program r

Opening Scene — Version one. Marcus types: "Turn these program reports into a summary document for the board."
NARRATION

Here is a real type of exchange that happens when professionals start using Cowork. Two people, same folder of program reports, same basic need. Version one. Marcus types: "Turn these program reports into a summary document for the board."

// SLIDE 04 — WHY A BRIEF IS NOT JUST A LONG

WHY A BRIEF IS NOT JUST A LONGER PROMPT.

Non-expert users trying to direct AI tools tend to rely on informal, conversational instructions and then troubleshoot f

Why a Brief Is Not Just a Longer Prompt — A task brief is a different kind of document. It defines the work before Cowork plans. It makes the delegation inspectab
NARRATION

Non-expert users trying to direct AI tools tend to rely on informal, conversational instructions and then troubleshoot failures after the fact rather than specifying requirements before (Zamfirescu-Pereira et al. 2023). The result is iterative trial-and-error , which wastes time and, in a Cowork context where files are being touched, can create a trail of partial outputs, modified files, and inconsistent drafts. A task brief is a different kind of document. It defines the work before Cowork plans. It makes the delegation inspectable.

// SLIDE 05 — THE SEVEN FIELDS

THE SEVEN FIELDS.

What outcome matters? This is the one-sentence purpose of the task — not the process, not the output artifact, but the p

The Seven Fields — Good: "Produce a board-ready summary of Q3 and Q4 2024 program results for an audience that has not read the original re
NARRATION

What outcome matters? This is the one-sentence purpose of the task , not the process, not the output artifact, but the purpose. Good: "Produce a board-ready summary of Q3 and Q4 2024 program results for an audience that has not read the original reports."

THE TEMPLATE

The Template — Objective:

NARRATION

Here is the full brief Priya wrote for the board summary from the opening scene: Objective: Produce a board-ready summary of Q3 and Q4 2024 program results for an audience that has not read the original reports and does not need full program detail.

// SLIDE 08 — WORKED EXAMPLE — MEETING NOTES

WORKED EXAMPLE — MEETING NOTES EXTRACTION

The brief template works equally well for extraction and summarization tasks. Here is a brief for converting a transcrip

Worked Example — Meeting Notes Extractio — Objective: Convert the attached meeting transcript into a structured summary for the project file: decisions, action ite
NARRATION

The brief template works equally well for extraction and summarization tasks. Here is a brief for converting a transcript into a structured meeting summary: Objective: Convert the attached meeting transcript into a structured summary for the project file: decisions, action items with owners, open questions.

// SLIDE 09 — THE HUMAN GATE FOR THIS CHAPTE

THE HUMAN GATE FOR THIS CHAPTER.

The brief is where your professional judgment enters the task before any execution begins. Exclusions require you to kno

The Human Gate for This Chapter — None of those judgments can be made by Cowork. They depend on your knowledge of the work, the audience, the constraints,
NARRATION

The brief is where your professional judgment enters the task before any execution begins. Exclusions require you to know which files should not be sources. Decision rules require you to know how conflicts should be handled. Stop conditions require you to know what gaps are unacceptable. None of those judgments can be made by Cowork. They depend on your knowledge of the work, the audience, the constraints, and the stakes. Writing the brief is not administrative overhead; it is where the human work happens.

// SLIDE 10 — COMMON MISTAKES

COMMON MISTAKES

Treating the brief as a longer version of a chat prompt.→Omitting exclusions because the folder is clean.→Leaving decision rules blank.→Skipping stop conditions for "low-risk" tasks.→Writing the output format as a preference rather than a requirement.
NARRATION

Treating the brief as a longer version of a chat prompt. A brief is a specification, not a conversation opener. Write it before Cowork responds, not as a back-and-forth. Omitting exclusions because the folder is clean. A clean folder still contains files that should not be source material. Exclusions are not about distrust; they are about precision.

// SLIDE 11 — AI WAYBACK MACHINE

AI WAYBACK MACHINE.

Grace Hopper→Run this:→"Grace Hopper COBOL compiler"→Now make the prompt better.
NARRATION

Grace Hopper spent decades translating between human intent and machine execution , first with early compilers, then with COBOL, a language designed so that business people, not only engineers, could specify what a computer should do. Her core conviction was that computers should do what humans mean, and that the job of the programmer was to make intent precise enough for the machine to follow without guessing. A Cowork task brief is the same project at a different level of abstraction.
